RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) and CISC (Complex Instruction Set Computer) represent two fundamentally different philosophies in processor design. RISC emphasizes simplicity and efficiency, aiming to reduce the number of instructions and make them as uniform as possible. CISC, on the other hand, provides a rich set of instructions that can perform complex operations in a single instruction, often at the cost of increased complexity and reduced performance potential.

**Instruction Set Design Philosophy**

RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) and CISC (Complex Instruction Set Computer) represent two fundamentally different approaches to instruction set architecture design. RISC emphasizes simplicity and uniformity, aiming to reduce the number of instructions and make them as regular and predictable as possible.
